decisions.summary: Endpoint path is GET /api/conversations/archived (dedicated subpath), not GET /api/conversations?archived=true.

Backend response shape is a unified DiscoveredSession-like row with a `source: 'managed-archived'` discriminator plus extra fields archivedAt and conversationName. Maps Conversation fields onto the existing Session interface so SessionTable/SessionDetail can render both managed-archived and discovered rows from one component tree.

DB join is done in conversations-db.ts via a new function listArchivedConversationsWithEnrichment() that LEFT JOINs discovered_sessions on conversations.claude_session_id = discovered_sessions.session_id. One SQL round trip; null-safe for archived conversations whose JSONL was never indexed.

Source filter chips live in FacetPanel (top of the panel, above the time-range filter): [All] [Discovered] [Managed-archived]. Default is 'All' (union of discovered + archived). 'Discovered' is the current list-discovered-sessions behavior. 'Managed-archived' calls the new endpoint.
Row keys in SessionTable use a composite of source + id (e.g., `archived-${id}` and `discovered-${id}`) to avoid React key collisions between the two tables sharing an id namespace.

Search (keyword/semantic) is disabled or shows a hint when source === 'managed-archived'. The search endpoints only know discovered_sessions; archived conversations are filtered locally by title only in 'Managed-archived' mode. Initial implementation hides the search bar when source === 'managed-archived'.

Anchor icon (lucide-react `Anchor`) marks managed-archived rows in SessionTable. Discovered rows keep their existing Star/CheckCircle/Circle indicators. The badge column is rendered for both row types from a single SessionTable; no shape duplication.

Unarchive action POSTs to existing /api/conversations/:name/unarchive (no new endpoint). On success it invalidates both the managed-archived list query and the active-conversations list query (so the Command Deck reflects it on its next read).
No new top-level Tab type is added to Header.tsx; no new sidebar entry; no separate ArchivedConversations page. Everything stays in the existing Sessions tab (ConversationsPage).

Backend route uses promisified fs/promises and the existing async patterns in conversations.ts. No execSync, readFileSync, or other sync FS calls. Origin validation reuses validateOrigin() like every other route in the file.

hazards.summary: conversations.claude_session_id may not match a discovered_sessions row if the scanner has not indexed the JSONL (or the JSONL no longer exists). The endpoint must LEFT JOIN and return null-safe fields when no enrichment row is found.
hazards.mitigation: Use LEFT JOIN. Default enrichmentLevel=0, messageCount=0, toolsUsed=[], filesTouched=[], tags=[], summary=null, estimatedCost=conversations.total_cost when discovered_sessions row is missing. Test covers this case.

hazards.summary: DiscoveredSession id and archived Conversation id share the integer namespace; rendering both in one table could collide on React keys.
hazards.mitigation: Use composite keys `${source}-${id}` for table rows. Selection state in ConversationsPage uses a tuple-like { source, id } selector (or stringified composite key).

hazards.summary: Search endpoints (pan.searchConversations) only know discovered_sessions; archived conversations are invisible to FTS/embeddings, so a global search behaves inconsistently when source='managed-archived' or 'all'.
hazards.mitigation: When source='managed-archived' hide the search bar (per D6). When source='all' search returns only discovered hits; explicitly note this in the UI via a small inline hint. Document as a follow-up for a future issue.

hazards.summary: Unarchive succeeds at the DB layer but the conversation's tmux session is gone (killed at archive). Command Deck reading 'active' would show the row but spawning into it requires resume, which may fail if the JSONL was also rotated.
hazards.mitigation: Out of scope here — unarchive only restores the DB row's archived_at to NULL. Command Deck's existing resume flow handles missing sessions; we don't change that behavior. AC explicitly says 'visible in Command Deck on next load/refresh', not 'attachable'.

hazards.summary: Conversations that were spawned with harness='pi' have JSONLs in ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/sessions/, NOT in ~/.claude/projects/. The discovered_sessions scanner only walks ~/.claude/projects/, so Pi conversations never get a discovered_sessions row.
hazards.mitigation: LEFT JOIN handles this naturally — Pi archived conversations come back with the conversation row only, no enrichment. The frontend renders them with title-as-summary. Pi conversations are rare in this view; the no-enrichment path is the expected default.

hazards.summary: ConversationsPage.test.tsx mocks the RPC client but the new archived list uses a REST endpoint (fetch). Existing test mocks won't intercept it without adding fetch mocking.
hazards.mitigation: Add a fetch mock (vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch')) in the new tests. Alternatively, abstract the archived-list call through an injectable fetcher. Prefer the direct fetch mock for symmetry with how other routes are exercised.

hazards.summary: Backend route module conversations.ts is 3009 lines. Adding a route at the top of the routes section (where getConversationsRoute lives) keeps related routes grouped, but the file is approaching unmaintainable. Do not refactor here; just add and register.
hazards.mitigation: Place the new getArchivedConversationsRoute immediately after getConversationsRoute (line ~1159). Register it in conversationsRouteLayer at the bottom (line ~2981).

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1391: Surface Panopticon-archived conversations in the Sessions tab
Auto-mode planning complete; vBRIEF written with 6 beads. Foundation beads (backend-endpoint, frontend-source-filter) flagged for inspection.
